discussion_comments.comment_body:
This breaks backward compatibility.
Suppose there is one old model file using the default alpha value. You will get different output with or w/o this change.
Have reverted it and add some comments instead.

```
axis = pd.get(3,0);
```

use 0 for caffe axis=1
use 1 for caffe axis=2

This is the axis convention in ncnn

eps should be added inside sqrt()
ref https://arxiv.org/abs/1502.03167

discussion_comments.comment_body:
as the non-fast path is not implemented

- disable support_packing based on ```fast_gelu``` value in create_pipeline()
- return ```GELU::forward``` if fast_gelu is zero

There isn't a ncnn layer called PairwiseDistance.
Either implement the layer or drop the ncnn conversion and leave pnnx conversion only.

If you plan to implement the ncnn layer later, add the ncnn conversion at that time.
